title: agoseris glauca var. villosa
intro: Agoseris glauca var. villosa, also known as hairy greasewood or hairy mountain dandelion, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Agoseris glauca var. villosa include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
